automotive-ev-battery.title: Germany: Enercity successfully sells electricity from EV batteries on the power market
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Enercity has successfully demonstrated vehicle-to-grid technology by supplying electricity from bidirectionally connected electric vehicles to the public grid in Hanover and trading it on the electricity exchange. This milestone achievement in a joint pilot with Volkswagen Commercial Vehicles marks the first real-world B2B fleet functioning as a virtual storage system within the energy market, generating revenue in the process.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Zum and SFUSD launch largest electric school bus fleet in the US
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Zum and San Francisco Unified School District have announced the deployment of the largest electric school bus fleet in the United States, comprising 238 electric buses integrated with bidirectional charging infrastructure and vehicle-to-grid capabilities. This major infrastructure project represents significant progress in electrifying public transportation and advancing grid-interactive charging technology.

automotive-ev-battery.title: V2G project: Hyundai Group and Vattenfall Test EVs as mobile energy storage
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Hyundai and Kia have announced a pilot project with Vattenfall to launch bidirectional charging in the Netherlands in the second half of the year, involving up to 80 households testing vehicle-to-grid technology with EV9 and Ioniq 9 vehicles over a six-month trial period. The initiative represents a significant step toward commercializing EV battery storage integration with power grids.

automotive-ev-battery.title: BYD Technology Strategy Highlights Hardware With China's First 4nm Intelligent Driving Chip
automotive-ev-battery.summary: BYD has launched its "Dare to Be" intelligent strategy and unveiled China's first 4nm intelligent driving chip, emphasizing the company's vertical integration from battery production to autonomous driving technology. Chairman Wang Chuanfu outlined BYD's vision that while electrification focuses on batteries, the next phase of intelligentization will center on advanced chip development.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Waymo to launch new Ojai robotaxi model for passenger service
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Robotaxi operator Waymo plans to integrate the new Ojai electric van model into its public transport service in the coming weeks, with the vehicle developed in collaboration with Chinese manufacturer Zeekr who provides the chassis and body. Waymo equips the vehicles with proprietary sensors, computers, and autonomous driving software for commercial deployment.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Over $55 Million Announced To Expand California Public EV Fast Charging
automotive-ev-battery.summary: California's Energy Commission announced $55.2 million in funding through the California Electric Vehicle Infrastructure Project to expand public EV fast charging infrastructure across the state. The investment addresses the current shortage of fast-charging stations despite California's inventory of over 201,000 public chargers, most of which are slower Level 2 chargers.

automotive-ev-battery.title: BMW opens configurator for rear-wheel-drive iX3
automotive-ev-battery.summary: BMW has opened the online configurator for the rear-wheel-drive version of its new Neue Klasse iX3, with customer orders available at a starting price of €63,400. Deliveries in Germany are scheduled to commence during the summer, marking an important milestone for BMW's new generation electric vehicle line.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Xpeng deliveries fall by one-third
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chinese EV manufacturer Xpeng reported a sharp decline in first-quarter 2026 deliveries, with 62,682 vehicles delivered representing a year-over-year drop of one-third and revenue declining 17.6 percent. The significant downturn highlights intensifying competition in China's electric vehicle market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Market launch of E7X: Audi's sub-brand makes its second move in China
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Audi has launched sales of the AUDI E7X, the second model for its sub-brand in China, a full-sized crossover SUV measuring five meters in length with up to 500 kilowatts of power. The vehicle is available in five variants with starting prices ranging from approximately €34,200 to €45,600.

automotive-ev-battery.title: How the Industrial Accelerator Act Can Help Avoid More Battery Factories Going Bust
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The European Union's Industrial Accelerator Act is examined as a potential solution to support struggling battery manufacturers, following the recent bankruptcy of Morrow Batteries and other EU battery start-ups. The analysis emphasizes the need for focused policy support on localizing and onshoring critical battery production capacity.

world-news.title: No deal announced after Trump meeting to make 'final determination' on Iran
world-news.summary: U.S. President Trump met with advisers following confirmation that the U.S. and Iran had agreed on a framework for a potential nuclear deal, though no formal agreement was announced. The talks represent a significant diplomatic development in U.S.-Iran relations with major implications for regional stability and nuclear proliferation.

world-news.title: Nato and EU condemn Russia after drone hits Romanian residential block
world-news.summary: NATO and the EU condemned Russia after a drone struck a residential building in Romania, with Romanian officials stating the Russian drone was likely intercepted over Ukraine before altering course. The incident marks an escalation in cross-border attacks affecting NATO territory during the ongoing Ukraine conflict.

world-news.title: Israel put on UN sexual violence in warzones blacklist for first time
world-news.summary: Israel was added to a UN blacklist for the first time regarding allegations of sexual violence in conflict zones, according to a report by the office of the UN secretary general. Israel has rejected the allegations contained in the report, which carries significant diplomatic and humanitarian implications.

world-news.title: 'Arbitrary measures': Lula slams US 'terror' designation for Brazil gangs
world-news.summary: Brazilian President Lula criticized the United States' decision to designate Brazilian criminal networks as terrorist organizations, warning that the move could undermine local law enforcement efforts. The dispute reflects tensions between Washington and Brasília over counterterrorism policy and jurisdictional authority.

world-news.title: Ghana parliament passes anti-LGBTQ+ bill
world-news.summary: Ghana's parliament passed legislation criminalizing same-sex acts and targeting individuals identifying as gay, lesbian, or transgender with potential jail sentences. The bill represents a significant policy shift with implications for human rights and international diplomatic relations in West Africa.

world-news.title: Iran denies ceasefire deal with US is 'finalised'
world-news.summary: Iran's Foreign Ministry denied that a ceasefire or nuclear agreement with the United States has been finalized, contradicting earlier reports of a framework agreement. The statement reflects ongoing diplomatic uncertainty in nuclear negotiations with significant global security implications.

world-news.title: Ebola treatment centre rebuilt after being torched by protesters in DRC
world-news.summary: Workers in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo are rebuilding an Ebola treatment centre that was destroyed by protesters earlier in the month. The incident highlights challenges in managing public health infrastructure and disease control efforts in conflict-affected regions.

world-news.title: Exploding rocket casts doubts over Nasa's Moon plans
world-news.summary: A Blue Origin rocket explosion has raised concerns about the viability of NASA's lunar program and commercial space development timelines. The incident represents a significant setback for U.S. space exploration ambitions and international efforts to return humans to the Moon.

world-news.title: Iran war live: Trump due to make 'final determination' on deal with Tehran
world-news.summary: As Trump prepares to make a final determination on Iran negotiations, Israel continues military operations deeper into Lebanon while ordering expanded occupation of Gaza. The simultaneous diplomatic and military developments create complex regional security dynamics with international ramifications.

world-news.title: 'UN: 100% of Gaza' should be for Palestinians
world-news.summary: A UN spokesperson stated that the entirety of Gaza territory should belong to Palestinians, representing an official UN position on Palestinian territorial rights. The statement reflects ongoing international debate over the Israeli-Palestinian conflict and territorial settlements.

finance-markets.title: US stocks post longest weekly winning streak since 2023
finance-markets.summary: US equity markets achieved their longest consecutive weekly gain streak since 2023, driven by renewed optimism surrounding artificial intelligence investments and diplomatic progress on extending a US-Iran ceasefire agreement. The rally reflects investor confidence in both technological advancement and potential geopolitical de-escalation.

finance-markets.title: S&P 500 Powers Historic Weekly Run on US-Iran Bets
finance-markets.summary: The S&P 500 extended its historic weekly winning streak on expectations that a ceasefire deal could resolve the Iran conflict, while the artificial-intelligence trade continued to provide market support. Investor sentiment has shifted materially toward geopolitical risk reduction and technology sector gains.

finance-markets.title: BoE governor warns Middle East ceasefire would 'still create uncertainty'
finance-markets.summary: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey cautioned that even a Middle East ceasefire would maintain macroeconomic uncertainty, signaling that interest rate cuts remain contingent on policymakers achieving greater confidence in the inflation and stability outlook. The statement tempers market expectations for near-term monetary easing in the UK.

finance-markets.title: Asia Rice Surges 20% in May as War and Weather Threaten Output
finance-markets.summary: Asian rice prices posted their largest monthly increase in nearly two decades during May, driven by weather-related production risks and war-induced spikes in energy and fertilizer costs. Further price appreciation is anticipated as supply-side pressures persist across major producing regions.

finance-markets.title: 'There's A Lot More To Come' In AI Says Aliaga
finance-markets.summary: JPMorgan Asset Management's Global Market Strategist Stephanie Aliaga projects continued upside potential in artificial intelligence markets, noting that the sector remains in its early stages with additional volatility expected as investors refine their valuations. The commentary underscores sustained momentum in technology-driven market segments.

finance-markets.title: Wall Street Week | Britain's Debt Problem, Poland's Economic Boom
finance-markets.summary: The UK's elevated debt burden and sluggish growth trajectory are rekindling concerns about potential financial market instability and policy disruption, while Poland has emerged as one of the world's fastest-expanding economies despite facing sustainability headwinds. Global investors are reassessing both developed-market fiscal risks and emerging-market growth dynamics.

finance-markets.title: SpaceX Wins $4 Billion Contract for US Golden Dome Satellites
finance-markets.summary: SpaceX has secured a contract exceeding $4 billion to manufacture satellites for the US military's Golden Dome defensive initiative, which is designed to track foreign aircraft and missiles. The award reflects continued government investment in space-based defense infrastructure under the Trump administration.

finance-markets.title: Quantinuum Said to Weigh Boosting IPO Size and Price Range
finance-markets.summary: Honeywell-backed quantum computing firm Quantinuum is considering enlarging its initial public offering scale and valuation range, signaling investor appetite for emerging technology ventures. The potential expansion reflects market enthusiasm for quantum computing's commercial applications and long-term value creation potential.

finance-markets.title: Citadel Securities Loses Court Fight Over New IEX Options Venue
finance-markets.summary: A federal appeals court rejected Citadel Securities' legal challenge to block IEX Group from launching a novel options exchange designed to intentionally slow trade execution. The ruling represents a significant market structure development affecting institutional trading practices and market maker competitiveness.

finance-markets.title: Milei to privatise Argentina's $10-a-night workers' hotels
finance-markets.summary: Argentina's libertarian government under President Milei is moving forward with privatization of the nation's extensive state-owned 'social tourism' hotel network, originally established under the Perón era. The initiative reflects the administration's broader agenda to reduce public sector holdings and transfer assets to private operators.

science-space.title: Incredible videos show Blue Origin rocket explosion could be seen from hundreds of miles away
science-space.summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket exploded during testing on May 28, producing a powerful blast visible across wide sections of Florida. The incident represents a significant setback for the company's heavy-lift launch vehicle program.

science-space.title: China's Shenzhou 21 astronauts return to Earth after being briefly 'stranded', wrapping up record-breaking mission
science-space.summary: China's Shenzhou 21 astronauts successfully returned to Earth on May 29 after completing a record-breaking mission to the Chinese space station. The mission was notable for overcoming technical challenges that temporarily affected crew operations.

science-space.title: Red dwarf stars are cosmic killers that eat their own planets
science-space.summary: Astronomers have identified the first observational evidence that red dwarf stars can consume their own planets, challenging previous assumptions about stellar behavior. This discovery has significant implications for understanding planetary survival and habitability around the galaxy's most common star type.

science-space.title: NASA's Roman Space Telescope Primary Mirror Gets Last Look
science-space.summary: NASA engineers at Goddard Space Flight Center have completed the final inspection of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope's 7.9-foot primary mirror, a critical component for the mission. The mirror will enable Roman to capture wide panoramic views of the universe and study cosmic phenomena across vast distances.

science-space.title: SpaceX launches 29 Starlink satellites from Florida just hours after Blue Origin rocket explosion
science-space.summary: SpaceX successfully launched 29 Starlink satellites from Cape Canaveral Space Force Station on May 29, demonstrating operational continuity at the facility following Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket explosion the previous day. The rapid turnaround showcases the space industry's ongoing momentum despite technical setbacks.

science-space.title: Atlas V rocket launches 29 Amazon Leo internet satellites to orbit from Florida
science-space.summary: A United Launch Alliance Atlas V rocket successfully deployed 29 Amazon Leo internet satellites to orbit on May 28 from Florida. The mission continues Amazon's expansion of its low-Earth orbit internet constellation.

science-space.title: NASA Awards Contract for Johnson Space Center Infrastructure
science-space.summary: NASA has selected seven companies to undertake construction and revitalization work at the Johnson Space Center in Houston under a contract valued up to $300 million. The improvements will modernize mission-support facilities, utilities, and equipment across the NASA Johnson campus, with all funds obligated by September 2026.

science-space.title: Rare Blue Moon hides the red light of an ancient star on May 31: Will you be able to see it?
science-space.summary: A rare second full moon in May will align with the red star Antares on May 31, creating a striking celestial viewing opportunity. The conjunction offers stargazers a chance to observe an intriguing astronomical event.

science-space.title: Hubble Spies Faint Irregular Galaxy
science-space.summary: The Hubble Space Telescope has captured detailed imagery of the dwarf irregular galaxy ESO 490-017, located approximately 23 million light-years away in the constellation Canis Major. The galaxy's low surface brightness and intricate structure provide insights into the diversity of galactic morphologies.

science-space.title: Painting the Growing Season in the Maize Triangle
science-space.summary: Agricultural radar data from South Africa's Maize Triangle region reveals detailed information about crop types and seasonal changes using advanced color-visualization techniques. The remote sensing approach demonstrates satellite technology's utility for monitoring agricultural productivity across large areas.

tech-ai.title: SpaceX awarded $6.45B in Space Force contracts ahead of IPO
tech-ai.summary: SpaceX has secured $6.45 billion in Space Force contracts as the company prepares for what could be the largest initial public offering ever. The aerospace company already generated one-fifth of its 2025 revenue from government contracts, demonstrating its critical role in U.S. defense infrastructure.

tech-ai.title: SpaceX gets $4 billion contract to build missile-tracking 'Golden Dome' satellites
tech-ai.summary: The Pentagon awarded SpaceX a $4.16 billion contract to develop missile-tracking satellites as part of President Trump's planned 'Golden Dome' defense system. The sensor-equipped satellites will enable the U.S. Space Force to detect and track targets from space, bolstering national defense capabilities.

tech-ai.title: Nvidia, Microsoft, and Arm are all teasing Nvidia's new N1X laptop processors
tech-ai.summary: Nvidia, Microsoft, and Arm are jointly teasing the imminent announcement of Nvidia's new Arm-powered N1X laptop processors, marking a significant shift in the PC processor market. The announcement is expected at Computex this weekend and represents what the companies are calling 'a new era of PC' computing.

tech-ai.title: Coders are refusing to work without AI — and that could come back to bite them
tech-ai.summary: While artificial intelligence is accelerating code production for software developers, research indicates that AI-generated code may not be higher quality, potentially creating long-term technical problems. Developers' increasing reliance on AI tools without scrutiny could compromise software reliability and maintainability.

tech-ai.title: What happens when companies become too AI-pilled?
tech-ai.summary: Executives making AI-driven workforce reduction decisions often lack deep understanding of the roles they are eliminating, according to Box founder Aaron Levie, who describes this trend as 'AI psychosis.' Companies like ClickUp have already cut 22 percent of their workforce for AI agents, while 2026 tech layoffs are nearly matching the total of all 2025.

tech-ai.title: Microsoft under fire for threatening security researcher with criminal investigation
tech-ai.summary: A public dispute between Microsoft and an independent security researcher has reignited debate over software security responsibilities in the tech industry. The incident highlights ongoing tensions between technology companies and the security research community regarding disclosure protocols and accountability.

tech-ai.title: After Nvidia's $20B not-acqui-hire, AI chip startup Groq reportedly raising $650M
tech-ai.summary: AI chip manufacturer Groq is seeking to raise $650 million in internal funding as it shifts focus from hardware development toward AI inference optimization. The funding round comes as the AI chipmaker landscape becomes increasingly competitive following Nvidia's major talent and technology acquisitions.

tech-ai.title: Cognition's Scott Wu says AI coding agents shouldn't replace humans
tech-ai.summary: Scott Wu, founder of Cognition which produces the AI coding agent Devin, clarifies that the technology is designed to augment rather than supplant human programmers. Despite Devin's success as a coding assistant, Wu emphasizes that human developers remain essential to software development.

tech-ai.title: Microsoft teases new Surface hardware and 'a new era of PC'
tech-ai.summary: Microsoft is teasing new Surface hardware announcements at its upcoming Build developer conference, with hints of innovative designs including curved display edges. The company confirmed that new announcements will target developers but are distinct from operating system updates.

tech-ai.title: Tech companies desperately want to film you doing chores
tech-ai.summary: AI training startups such as Shift are offering free home cleaning services while recording footage of household labor to build datasets for training AI models. The practice raises privacy concerns as companies seek to capture and monetize everyday activities for artificial intelligence development.

health-biotech.title: At ASCO, positive data for Bristol in multiple myeloma and Pfizer in lung cancer
health-biotech.summary: Bristol Myers Squibb and Pfizer presented positive clinical trial data at the American Society of Clinical Oncology annual meeting, with Bristol demonstrating efficacy in multiple myeloma treatment and Pfizer showing promising results in lung cancer therapy. These presentations represent significant advances in oncology treatment options for patients.

health-biotech.title: Bristol Myers details Phase 3 trial win for Pomalyst successor
health-biotech.summary: Bristol Myers Squibb announced a Phase 3 trial victory for its next-generation multiple myeloma drug, which demonstrated a 52% reduction in disease progression or death risk when combined with standard treatment regimens. The result reinforces the company's position in the competitive multiple myeloma market.

health-biotech.title: Pfizer strikes Innovent deal worth up to $10.5B for a dozen cancer programs
health-biotech.summary: Pfizer announced a strategic partnership with China-based Innovent Biologics valued at up to $10.5 billion to develop and commercialize a portfolio of 12 early-stage cancer therapy candidates, including antibody-drug conjugates and multi-specific antibodies. The deal underscores Pfizer's commitment to expanding its oncology pipeline through external innovation.

health-biotech.title: Trump administration seeks to overhaul federal grantmaking process, alarming researchers
health-biotech.summary: The Trump administration has proposed sweeping changes to regulations governing federal grants, raising concerns among the research community about potential impacts on scientific funding and research independence. The proposal affects the foundational framework for all federal research funding.

health-biotech.title: Next FDA user fee deal is under review at the White House
health-biotech.summary: The White House is currently reviewing a negotiated agreement between the FDA and the biopharmaceutical industry that establishes prescription drug user fees for fiscal years 2028-2032. The review suggests potential scrutiny of the longstanding fee structure that supports FDA regulatory operations.

health-biotech.title: Replimune will again submit its melanoma drug for approval
health-biotech.summary: Replimune announced plans to submit its advanced melanoma candidate RP1 for FDA approval for the third time, following new discussions with the regulatory agency after two prior rejections. The company believes it has addressed previous concerns and is proceeding with a new submission.

health-biotech.title: Which vaccines will curb the Ebola outbreak? WHO debates
health-biotech.summary: The World Health Organization is evaluating vaccine strategies to control the expanding Ebola outbreak originating in northeastern Congo, weighing efficacy and deployment considerations. The selection of appropriate vaccine candidates remains a critical decision amid the ongoing public health emergency.

health-biotech.title: Massachusetts sues UnitedHealth, alleging fraud in state's Medicaid plans for seniors
health-biotech.summary: The Massachusetts Attorney General has filed a lawsuit against UnitedHealthcare, alleging the company fraudulently misrepresented member health status to boost profits in state Medicaid programs serving seniors. The allegations suggest systematic upcoding of patient severity to increase reimbursement rates.

health-biotech.title: Opinion: I'm a weight-loss doctor. Here's why I worry about GLP-1 'microdoses'
health-biotech.summary: A weight-loss medicine physician expresses concerns about the growing off-label use of GLP-1 medications in microdoses for cosmetic weight loss, citing insufficient long-term safety data for these lower dosing regimens. The commentary highlights the gap between clinical evidence and emerging consumer practices in GLP-1 utilization.

health-biotech.title: AstraZeneca seeks path for failed antibody; Imfinzi, Tremfya get label expansions
health-biotech.summary: AstraZeneca is exploring alternative development pathways for its monoclonal antibody anselamimab following Phase 3 trial failures, while simultaneously advancing label expansions for existing oncology and immunology products Imfinzi and Tremfya. The company's strategy reflects efforts to salvage value from failed programs while expanding approved indications.

crypto-web3.title: U.S. CFTC opens crypto 'perp' door with first approvals at Kalshi, Coinbase
crypto-web3.summary: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission has granted landmark regulatory approval for crypto perpetual futures contracts, with Coinbase and Kalshi receiving the first licenses to offer these products to U.S. traders. This approval marks a significant shift in regulatory stance and brings one of the largest unregulated crypto markets under U.S. oversight.

crypto-web3.title: Paxos wins SEC approval to clear U.S. stocks on blockchain
crypto-web3.summary: Paxos has become the first and only blockchain-native firm to secure SEC registration as a clearing and settlement agency, positioning it as a direct competitor to legacy infrastructure providers such as the DTCC. This regulatory milestone enables Paxos to offer more efficient blockchain-based settlement services for traditional securities.

crypto-web3.title: 'The banks will not accept it': Dimon escalates battle over stablecoin rewards in CLARITY Act debate
crypto-web3.summary: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon escalated his criticism of the proposed CLARITY Act, warning that banks will reject a framework allowing stablecoin issuers to offer yield-bearing rewards similar to bank deposits. The dispute highlights a fundamental disagreement between traditional finance and crypto firms over regulatory structure for digital assets.

crypto-web3.title: US has seized nearly $1 billion in crypto from Iran, Bessent says
crypto-web3.summary: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent disclosed that the country has seized nearly $1 billion in cryptocurrency assets from Iran, nearly doubling a previous estimate of $500 million made earlier in the year. The seizure represents a significant enforcement action against sanctions evasion through digital assets.

crypto-web3.title: Clarity Act Risks Regulation Without Oversight, Brookings Fellow Says
crypto-web3.summary: Aaron Klein, a Brookings Institution fellow, cautioned that the proposed CLARITY Act could establish crypto market regulation without adequate oversight mechanisms, arguing the CFTC requires additional resources, independence, and cross-agency coordination. His analysis underscores structural concerns about the regulatory framework under legislative debate.

crypto-web3.title: Hyperliquid bigger than NASDAQ, says ICE CEO Jeffrey Sprecher
crypto-web3.summary: Intercontinental Exchange CEO Jeffrey Sprecher praised Hyperliquid's trading platform, noting it has surpassed NASDAQ in trading volume and signaling Wall Street incumbents' growing engagement with crypto-native infrastructure. The statement reflects the accelerating convergence between traditional finance and decentralized crypto markets.

crypto-web3.title: Aave Labs secures dual UK licenses for regulated crypto payments infrastructure through local subsidiary
crypto-web3.summary: Aave Labs obtained FCA cryptoasset registration in the United Kingdom for its Push subsidiaries, enabling zero-fee fiat on-ramps and establishing regulated crypto payment infrastructure in a major jurisdiction. This regulatory achievement expands Aave's compliance footprint and enables broader institutional adoption.

crypto-web3.title: What American crypto asset perpetuals mean for the future of crypto
crypto-web3.summary: CFTC Chairman Selig explains the strategic implications of U.S. approval for crypto perpetual futures contracts, noting that bringing this previously offshore-dominated market under American regulation fundamentally changes the landscape for digital assets. The regulatory shift is expected to accelerate institutional adoption and market maturation.

crypto-web3.title: Base launches Azul on mainnet, pushing Coinbase's Ethereum L2 toward full decentralization
crypto-web3.summary: Coinbase's Layer 2 blockchain Base has deployed its Azul upgrade on mainnet, implementing multiproofs and a new client stack to advance toward full decentralization. The upgrade represents progress toward reducing Base's reliance on centralized sequencers and infrastructure.

crypto-web3.title: Mass deployment of AI agents is a disaster waiting to happen, says CertiK CEO
crypto-web3.summary: CertiK CEO Ronghui Gu warned of security risks from widespread AI agent deployment in crypto and blockchain environments, offering guidance on isolation testing protocols to prevent unauthorized access to sensitive personal data and digital assets. The warning highlights emerging security concerns as AI integration accelerates in decentralized systems.

startups-vc.title: Anthropic Nears $1T Valuation And Leapfrogs OpenAI On Unicorn Board With $65B Funding Round
startups-vc.summary: Generative AI company Anthropic announced a $65 billion Series H funding round, more than doubling its post-money valuation to $965 billion and positioning it as one of the most valuable private companies globally. The mega-round underscores the concentration of venture capital in leading AI companies and marks a significant milestone in the AI funding landscape.

startups-vc.title: After Nvidia's $20B not-acqui-hire, AI chip startup Groq reportedly raising $650M
startups-vc.summary: AI chip startup Groq is seeking to raise $650 million in funding as it shifts its strategic focus from hardware development to AI inference optimization. The fundraising round reflects growing investor interest in specialized AI infrastructure beyond traditional compute.

startups-vc.title: This chip startup just raised $135M on a bet that AI's biggest bottleneck isn't compute — it's memory
startups-vc.summary: South Korean chip startup XCENA has raised $135 million, betting that memory constraints rather than computational power represent the primary limitation for AI systems. The funding validates an emerging thesis among infrastructure investors that specialized memory solutions will be critical to advancing AI capabilities.

startups-vc.title: Exclusive: Capchase, The 'Affirm for B2B,' Secures $200M In Debt And Equity
startups-vc.summary: B2B financing startup Capchase has secured $200 million in combined debt and equity funding, consisting of $26 million in equity and a $174 million credit facility. The funding reflects growing demand for flexible financing solutions tailored to software-as-a-service companies and other subscription-based business models.

startups-vc.title: Cognition's Scott Wu says AI coding agents shouldn't replace humans
startups-vc.summary: Scott Wu, founder of Cognition and creator of Devin, the leading AI coding agent, has stated that such tools are designed to complement rather than replace human programmers. Wu's positioning reflects ongoing industry debate about the appropriate role of AI agents in software development workflows.

startups-vc.title: Does your CEO have AI psychosis? Aaron Levie thinks most of them do.
startups-vc.summary: Box founder Aaron Levie has criticized corporate leadership for making AI-driven workforce decisions without adequately understanding job functions, coining the term "AI psychosis" to describe this phenomenon. Recent tech industry layoffs justified by AI adoption suggest a broader pattern of strategic decisions made without sufficient domain expertise.

startups-vc.title: They Saw Women Shut Out Of VC, So A PayPal Veteran And Former Navy Officer Built An Alternative
startups-vc.summary: Co-founders Molly Huyck and Amie Konwinski have launched Aequitas Invest, a funding portal designed to address the persistent underrepresentation of women-led businesses in venture capital. The platform aims to improve capital access and equity retention for female entrepreneurs within the startup ecosystem.

startups-vc.title: Crunchbase Data: Venture Dollars For Black Startup Founders Stay Scarce Despite AI Funding Boom
startups-vc.summary: Despite a modest uptick in overall venture funding in 2025, the proportion of capital directed to companies with Black founders remained persistently low. The data highlights ongoing structural disparities in venture capital allocation even as AI funding accelerates across the broader ecosystem.

startups-vc.title: Navigating The DPI Crunch And Startup Funding
startups-vc.summary: Guest author Marc Schröder advises founders to evaluate investors' financial health and distribution potential alongside their market expertise, noting that acquisition-focused exits currently dominate over IPOs in venture-backed M&A activity. Strategic founder diligence on investor fundamentals has become increasingly important in the current funding environment.

startups-vc.title: The IPO Comeback Has A Catch
startups-vc.summary: As initial public offering requirements have increased substantially, many mid-sized companies lack viable exit pathways, creating demand for a more mature private secondary market. This structural gap in exit opportunities has become a pressure point for venture-backed companies and their shareholders.

cybersecurity.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
cybersecurity.summary: A major data extortion attack targeting the Canvas education technology platform disrupted classes across U.S. schools and universities, with a cybercrime group defacing login pages and threatening to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 institutions. The attack represents a significant threat to critical educational infrastructure and affects millions of individuals nationwide.

cybersecurity.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
cybersecurity.summary: Major software vendors including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla, and Oracle are releasing near record volumes of security patches this month, with AI-powered tools proving effective at identifying vulnerabilities in human-written code. The accelerated patching tempo reflects both the growing sophistication of threats and the increasing role of artificial intelligence in security research.

cybersecurity.title: Malicious Sicoob NuGet Steals Banking Credentials as npm Packages Target Cloud Secrets
cybersecurity.summary: Researchers discovered a malicious NuGet package impersonating a C# SDK for Sicoob, a major Brazilian cooperative financial system, designed to exfiltrate client IDs and PFX certificates used for authentication. The attack highlights the ongoing threat of supply chain compromises targeting software package repositories.

cybersecurity.title: Kimsuky Deploys HTTPSpy, Expands Arsenal with HelloDoor and VS Code Tunnels
cybersecurity.summary: The North Korean state-sponsored threat actor Kimsuky launched targeted cyber attacks against South Korean military and corporate entities in March and April 2026, employing sophisticated social engineering tactics including spoofed security software and fake meeting pages. The campaign demonstrates the evolving sophistication of state-sponsored threat actors targeting critical government and commercial infrastructure.

cybersecurity.title: New Russian-Linked GREYVIBE Targets Ukraine with AI-Powered Cyberattacks
cybersecurity.summary: A previously undocumented Russian-speaking threat actor designated GREYVIBE has been conducting persistent attacks against Ukraine and Ukrainian-related entities since August 2025, with activities aligned to Kremlin state interests. The campaign represents a significant geopolitical cyber threat leveraging advanced tactics and artificial intelligence capabilities.

cybersecurity.title: ChatGPhish Vulnerability Turns ChatGPT Web Summaries Into a Phishing Surface
cybersecurity.summary: Cybersecurity researchers disclosed a vulnerability in ChatGPT's web summaries feature that leverages the AI assistant's trust in Markdown links to enable prompt injection attacks and phishing operations. The technique, codenamed ChatGPhish by Permiso Security, demonstrates how AI assistants can be weaponized to compromise users.

cybersecurity.title: ChatGPT share links abused to host fake outage pages to deliver malware
cybersecurity.summary: Threat actors are abusing ChatGPT's content-sharing feature to create fraudulent OpenAI outage pages that deceive users into downloading malware disguised as the ChatGPT desktop application. The attack exploits user trust in legitimate platforms and demonstrates the dual-use risks of widely-adopted sharing features.

cybersecurity.title: With Complex Cloud Integrations, Small Errors Lead to Major Compromises
cybersecurity.summary: Researchers identified a critical exploit chain affecting a popular automation service that combines over-permissioned roles, secrets discovery, and non-human identity misconfigurations to enable full system compromise. The vulnerability chain underscores the compounding risks of misconfigured cloud environments and excessive privilege assignments.

cybersecurity.title: Attackers Use LLM Agent for Post-Exploitation After Marimo CVE-2026-39987 Exploit
cybersecurity.summary: An unknown threat actor exploited the Marimo CVE-2026-39987 vulnerability on internet-facing systems and subsequently deployed a large language model agent to conduct post-compromise actions, including extraction of cloud credentials. The incident represents an emerging threat pattern combining traditional vulnerability exploitation with advanced AI-powered post-exploitation techniques.

cybersecurity.title: What 2,000 Exposed Vibe-Coded Apps Reveal About the Limits of Most Security Stacks
cybersecurity.summary: Security researchers discovered approximately 2,000 exposed AI-generated applications that were deployed to production environments without proper security or IT oversight, representing a significant new attack surface. The findings highlight organizational risks from shadow AI development and the growing challenge of securing unsanctioned employee-built applications.

energy-climate.title: AI boom means US is now 'investing more' in fossil-fuel power than China
energy-climate.summary: The rapid expansion of data centers driven by artificial intelligence is spurring increased investment in gas-fired power generation in the United States, surpassing China's fossil fuel power investments. This trend underscores a critical tension in the energy transition as energy-intensive AI infrastructure creates new demand for dispatchable generation sources.

energy-climate.title: EM-DAT: Trump aid cuts could close database storing 'world's memory of disasters'
energy-climate.summary: Proposed cuts to U.S. foreign aid threaten to close EM-DAT, the world's most comprehensive disaster database relied upon by thousands of climate scientists and researchers globally. The potential loss of this critical resource would significantly impair climate research, disaster preparedness, and evidence-based climate policy development.

energy-climate.title: Entergy's gas projects are one-third of MISO's fast-track interconnection process
energy-climate.summary: Entergy Corporation's proposed capacity additions, approximately 70 percent of which are designed to serve planned data centers in Louisiana and Mississippi, account for one-third of MISO's expedited interconnection queue. This development illustrates how data center expansion is driving significant natural gas infrastructure investments across the regional grid operator.

energy-climate.title: India installs 2.7 GW of rooftop PV in Q1
energy-climate.summary: India achieved 2.7 gigawatts of rooftop solar capacity installations in the first quarter of 2026, with residential installations accounting for 82 percent of additions under the government's PM Surya Ghar program. This substantial growth demonstrates accelerating momentum in India's distributed solar deployment and residential clean energy adoption.

energy-climate.title: Chinese researchers build 33.33%-efficient perovskite-silicon tandem solar cell via new passivation strategy
energy-climate.summary: Chinese researchers at the Chinese Academy of Sciences developed a perovskite-silicon tandem solar cell achieving 33.33 percent efficiency using an innovative peak-selective passivation strategy with aluminum oxide nanocoating. This breakthrough represents significant progress in next-generation solar cell technology that could substantially improve photovoltaic conversion efficiency.

energy-climate.title: Antora, POET commission 5GWh thermal battery system in South Dakota
energy-climate.summary: Antora Energy and POET have commissioned a 5-gigawatt-hour thermal energy storage system at POET's bioprocessing facility in South Dakota, advancing long-duration energy storage capabilities. This deployment demonstrates the commercial viability of thermal battery technology for industrial applications requiring extended discharge periods.

energy-climate.title: SolaX ORI energy storage system completes large-scale fire test under extreme conditions
energy-climate.summary: The SolaX ORI energy storage system has successfully completed comprehensive large-scale fire testing under extreme fault conditions, advancing industry safety standards beyond component-level assessments. This validation demonstrates the importance of evaluating complete battery energy storage systems under realistic and severe operational scenarios to ensure grid reliability.

energy-climate.title: Qualitas Energy funds 211MW battery projects in Italy
energy-climate.summary: Qualitas Energy has secured financing for a 211-megawatt battery energy storage system portfolio in Italy, expanding energy storage capacity in the European market. This investment supports Italy's grid modernization and renewable energy integration objectives amid the continent's accelerating clean energy transition.

energy-climate.title: DeBriefed 29 May 2026: Europe's 'mind-boggling' May | Indian heat deaths | Nigeria's solar mini-grids
energy-climate.summary: This weekly digest covers significant climate and energy developments including exceptional temperature anomalies in Europe, mortality impacts from extreme heat in India, and distributed solar expansion in Nigeria. The compilation highlights the intersection of climate impacts, energy access, and renewable energy deployment across diverse global regions.

energy-climate.title: The Hydrogen Stream: JCB launches hydrogen-powered backhoe loader
energy-climate.summary: JCB has launched its first hydrogen-powered backhoe loader as part of a £100 million hydrogen engine development program, featuring a 55-kilowatt hydrogen engine designed to deliver comparable performance to diesel equivalents. This commercial deployment of hydrogen fuel cell technology in heavy equipment demonstrates progress toward decarbonizing the construction and industrial machinery sectors.

asia-pacific.title: Rising costs in Singapore spur business migration as regional alternatives rise
asia-pacific.summary:
Recent moves by companies such as Gardenia and H&M to shift some operations from Singapore across the Causeway have put the spotlight on Malaysia’s growing appeal as a more affordable base for businesses facing high costs and a tight labour market in the city state.
But while the trend may look like an easy win for its neighbour – bringing investment, jobs and spillover opportunities – analysts say the gains could come with trade-offs, as an influx of firms drives up competition for skilled...

asia-pacific.title: Taxi trade fears 10% could lose cabs as drivers gear up for ride-hailing
asia-pacific.summary:
Hong Kong’s taxi trade has warned that 10 per cent of the cab owners in the city could soon default on vehicle mortgages as licence values plunge, with many drivers switching to the new ride-hailing regime where 10,000 permits will be issued.
The market value of a taxi licence has plunged over the past two decades, from a historic high of HK$7.66 million (US$970,163) in 2009 to HK$2.62 million for urban or red cabs and HK$1.45 million for green or New Territories taxis as of May this year,...

asia-pacific.title: Recent analysis shows Neanderthals may have been first dentists in recorded history
asia-pacific.summary:
One of the most dreaded terms in dentistry is “root canal,” but at least we get to enjoy the perks of modern medicine. Now, imagine enduring that painful process nearly 60,000 years ago.
An international team of scientists believes they have found evidence that Neanderthals, the closest relatives of modern humans, deliberately drilled a hole in a lower molar tooth to remove infected tissue.
The discovery represents the oldest known example of invasive dental treatment, and it occurred tens of...

asia-pacific.title: Shangri-La Dialogue: Hegseth addresses China-US relations post-Trump visit to Beijing
asia-pacific.summary:

Defence ministers, military chiefs, diplomats and analysts have gathered in Singapore this weekend for the annual Shangri-La Dialogue, where regional security challenges will be discussed and a flurry of bilateral meetings held.
It is expected to provide the first indication of US policy direction..

asia-pacific.title: Trump ordered to address fraud claims in IRS case that led to ‘slush fund’ for allies
asia-pacific.summary:
A federal judge ordered US President Donald Trump to respond to “grievous allegations” that his deal with the government to create a US$1.8 billion fund to resolve his lawsuit against the Internal Revenue Service was the result of a “fraud” on the court.
In an order on Friday, US District Judge Kathleen Williams gave the president’s personal lawyers until June 12 to respond to a request by a group of former federal judges that she reopen the proceedings to investigate conduct by the president...

asia-pacific.title: Tariffs, war and broken trust: Southeast Asia’s Trump problem
asia-pacific.summary:
The goodbye emails started arriving on a Monday. By Friday, Sheila’s inbox told the story of an organisation coming apart at the seams, one farewell at a time.
Sheila* had spent nearly a decade helping run US-funded development projects in the Philippines. She had watched them grow, hire staff and take root in local communities.
When Washington ordered a freeze on foreign aid in January 2025, she told herself it was just a pause – a bureaucratic blip pending a review by the new...

asia-pacific.title: Why the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation isn’t Nato for Iran
asia-pacific.summary:
Central Asia is tilting more decisively towards China as geopolitical uncertainty deepens, with Beijing’s expanding influence recasting the former Soviet states’ strategic orientation. In the final instalment of a three-part series, Cao Jiaxuan examines how Central Asian members of the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation have shaped the bloc’s response to the US-Iran conflict. Read the first part here and the second part here.
As Kyrgyzstan gears up to host the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation...

asia-pacific.title: Why the Philippines walks a delicate balance as Asean chair
asia-pacific.summary:
When Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jnr accepted the Asean gavel from Malaysian counterpart Anwar Ibrahim in Kuala Lumpur last October, the script was already written: a packed agenda, South China Sea diplomacy in the spotlight and a regional digital economy deal to clinch. Then the world changed.
On February 28, US and Israeli forces struck Iran. The Strait of Hormuz, an artery of global shipping through which 98 per cent of the Philippines’ crude oil imports travel, became a war zone...

asia-pacific.title: Ex-California mayor Eileen Wang pleads guilty to acting as China agent
asia-pacific.summary:
A former Southern California mayor pleaded guilty on Friday to acting as an illegal agent of the Chinese government.
Eileen Wang, who stepped down as the mayor of Arcadia earlier this month, was charged in April with one count of acting in the US as an illegal agent of a foreign government.
She pleaded guilty to doing the bidding of Chinese officials by sharing articles favourable to Beijing, without prior notification to the US government as required by law.
The 56-year-old was elected in...

asia-pacific.title: US judge orders Trump’s name removed from Kennedy Centre
asia-pacific.summary:
⁠A judge on Friday ordered the removal of US President Donald Trump’s name from the Kennedy Centre for the ‌Performing Arts, ruling that the iconic Washington venue cannot be renamed without an act of Congress.
US District Judge Christopher Cooper in Washington directed the ⁠Trump administration to take down all physical signage ‌bearing Trump’s name and to eliminate any references to a “Trump Kennedy ‌Centre” from official materials within 14 ⁠days.
“The ⁠Kennedy Centre’s organic statute makes.
